# How to Grow Cannabis in 10 Easy Steps
by Nebula Haze
**Table of Contents**
[Introduction: How to Get to Harvest in 10 Steps](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#cannabis-growing-information-kit)
Step 1: [Choose Your Place to Grow](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#Step-1-Choose-Indoors-or-Outdoors)
Step 2: [Choose Your Light](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#Step-2-Choose-Your-Grow-Light)
Step 3: [Choose Your Growing Medium](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#Step-3-Choose-Your-Growing-Medium)
Step 4: [Get Cannabis-Friendly Nutrients](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#Step-4-Choose-Your-Nutrients)
Step 5: [Get Good Seeds (or Clones)](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#Step-5-Get-Your-Weed-Plants-Seeds-OR-Clones)
Step 6: [Germinate Your Seeds / Start Your Clones](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#Step-6-How-to-Germinate-Weed-Seeds)
Step 7: [Vegetative Stage – Only Stems and Leaves](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#Step-7-Vegetative-Stage)
Step 8: [Flowering Stage – Buds Start Growing!](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#Step-8-Flowering-Stage)

## **  
Your Growing Cannabis Information Kit:**
## How to Grow Great Weed in 10 Steps!
Cannabis legalization is spreading like wildfire across the US, Canada, and in many other countries around the world. Many people are finally allowed to **legally** grow their own supply of cannabis!
_Are you ready to grow your own weed at home?_
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/home-grown-cannabis-big-marijuana-buds-in-hand.jpg)
Growing cannabis can seem like it’s complicated, but often it only seems that way because you haven’t been given the right information. A lot of people unintentionally make growing harder than it needs to be, but that ends now!
This cannabis growing guide will help you discover the best way to grow cannabis, for _your_ unique situation.
Find a grow style for…
  * Your grow space
  * Your budget
  * Your desired yields

Growing cannabis plants is actually pretty straightforward, and almost anyone with a few extra minutes a day and a spare closet or a garden in the backyard can grow their own professional-quality buds at home.
**What does a cannabis plant need to thrive?**
In order to thrive and grow, every cannabis plant needs:
  1. **Light** – whether you’re using sunlight or grow lights, you must understand the light needs of a cannabis plant to get the best bud quality & yields.
  2. **Growing Medium** – the stuff your plants grow in; soil isn’t your only choice!
  3. **Air** – a well-ventilated space with good air exchange and a slight breeze is best.
  4. **Temperature** – A good rule of thumb for cannabis plants is if it feels too hot for you, it’s probably too hot for your plants. Just like humans, cannabis plants can die if exposed to extreme temps.
  5. **Nutrients** – you can buy pre-formulated nutrients that you just add to your water, or you can compost your own super soil so that it already includes all the nutrients you need.
  6. **Water** – like all plants and living creatures, cannabis needs water to survive and grow. [Is my tap water “good enough” for growing cannabis?](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#water-good-enough)

When growing cannabis indoors or outdoors, you will need to ensure that it gets the proper amount of these 6 resources.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/05/healthy-marijuana-leaf.jpg)
**How long does it take to grow cannabis?**
If you planted a cannabis seed today, when is the _soonest_ you could be actually _smoking_ your harvest? Probably about **2 months** with a quick-finishing [autoflowering strain](https://www.growweedeasy.com/autoflowering-vs-photoperiod). (Learn how to [get to harvest as fast as possible](https://www.growweedeasy.com/how-to-get-to-harvest-as-fast-as-possible)!)
Indoor grows tend to be shorter than outdoor grows since you have more control over when the plant starts budding. [Auto-flowering](https://www.growweedeasy.com/autoflowering-vs-photoperiod) grows also tend to be very short. But with shorter grows, you also tend to get smaller yields. Certain strains and certain outdoor grows can take up to 7 months or more.
On average, I’d say it takes a grower about **3 – 5 months** to grow, harvest, and be holding ready-to-smoke buds in hand.
[Learn more about how long it takes to grow cannabis](https://www.growweedeasy.com/how-long-does-it-take-to-grow-weed).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2010/04/sundial-time.jpg)
Don’t Make the 3 Most Common Cannabis Growing Mistakes!
  1. **Random experiments****–** One of the most common mistakes by new cannabis growers is conducting spur-of-the-moment experiments that hurt or possibly even kill their plants. Always take a second to google your idea before you try it. Luckily when it comes to growing cannabis, there is a good chance that someone has tried it already!
  2. **Skipping important steps** – The next most common problem new growers have is the tendency to skip crucial steps like understanding [light schedules](https://www.growweedeasy.com/light-schedules), or why [root pH levels are important for reducing nutrient deficiencies](https://www.growweedeasy.com/ph). While you _can_ get lucky and succeed at growing weed without taking these steps, you are a lot more likely to end up with plants that are sickly or don’t produce well. Make sure you follow all the steps listed in this guide – you deserve to harvest your own top-quality bud!
  3. **They don’t follow a proven guide** – Don’t re-invent the wheel! If you follow the steps in this guide, you’ll grow great weed. Yes, even if it’s your very first grow and you’ve never grown a plant in your life. You can benefit from the mistakes of other growers and learn not to make the same mistakes. And of course, don’t be afraid to look something up or [ask a fellow grower](https://forum.growweedeasy.com/).

## **Step 1: Choose Where You Will Grow (Indoors or Outdoors)**
  * [Indoors](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#indoors)
  * [Outdoors](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#outdoors)

_[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2018/09/flowering-marijuana-plants-315-cmh-grow-light.jpg)**Indoor Growing**_
Growing indoors is much more private than growing outdoors and you also get more control over your grow.
Indoor cannabis grow can be surprisingly cheap to get started and maintain, especially if you plan on growing just a few plants.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/cannabis-plants-growing-in-a-DIY-closet-setup.jpg)
Take a look at a few completed [indoor cannabis grow journals](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-grow-journals) and my [personal cannabis grow journals](https://www.growweedeasy.com/side-by-side-cannabis-grow-journal-experiments-by-nebula-haze) to get an idea of how much you can expect to harvest in different types of indoor setups. For example, in [this recent grow journal](https://www.growweedeasy.com/led-grow-journal-mars-hydro-tsl-2000w-vs-spider-farmer-sf-2000), my [200W Spider Farmer](https://www.amazon.com/Spider-Farmer-SF2000-Dimmable-Hydroponics/dp/B07TVD1Y3K?crid=20RT5EHI4X4T&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.Mj8ccUG3EWZvYxWkcwdhIzOWyEthCc1YoP1wFZwqXeXYDl2CNz4E1lcS5ZhLkiEEXqjd4YAXI5dPdbsIX3t6H8FvZSHXd-CewXnvvC0vxnPIl6cIS5YzMmjQ7--1RDQv3JV4hTngNR5NH9C-TzylZPLcgiShQxYAOlkhQxikpdoxc42Sai1wkhHObfFEyci-qWNo2OpwdhpNUXn_6hTSBlba1srJsoBvXzjcQ5KoUdKD9SEJocdMp7refLeR1yYkoA-1Ubp9s1b2Eb1LqhBvvVD9HZZ2uggTaOPoKlsUuiA.8PTPZIQ-cWu5m4d3fvlLWVstJ4j6-T5KQhJFzb-_Acs&dib_tag=se&keywords=spider%2Bfarmer%2Bsf2000&qid=1738968990&sprefix=spider%2Bfarmer%2Bsf2000%2Caps%2C196&sr=8-1-spons&sp_csd=d2lkZ2V0TmFtZT1zcF9hdGY&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=f6aa590577026f6c5bf330a77d27fd44&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) LED grow light yielded 302.3 grams (over 10 oz) of dried and cured cannabis.
You have more control over everything in an indoor growing environment, which means that indoor growers can consistently produce dank buds. However, this dank weed-growing power comes with more responsibility. As an indoor grower, your plants are 100% reliant on you your care if they are to survive. If you don’t provide everything your plants need, they will die.
_What space works best to grow cannabis?_
You can grow cannabis almost anywhere that has easy access to water and fresh air…
  * a spare room
  * a closet
  * garage
  * [grow tent](https://www.growweedeasy.com/cannabis-grow-tent) – _highly recommended!_ (a grow tent makes life easy)
  * extra bathroom
  * even the inside of a computer case!  
(though I recommend a [Space Bucket](https://www.growweedeasy.com/marijuana-space-buckets) if you want to go the “micro-grow” route:)

A grow tent is perfectly suited to grow cannabis, and a [small grow tent](https://www.growweedeasy.com/mini-grow-tent-shopping-list-tutorial-100w-led-grow-light) can fit almost anywhere.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/2x2x3-mini-tent-flowering-plants-sm.jpg)
When thinking about where to grow indoors, you should also consider the temperature (also referred to as ‘temps’) of your grow space and remember your temps will rise once you have your grow lights running!.

When plants are a bit older, in the budding/flowering stage, it’s best to keep temps slightly cooler, around 65-80°F (18-26°C) to produce buds with the best color, trichome production and smell.
Because temperature is so important, it’s best to be able to have some amount of control over the temperature of your grow area. When growing indoors, your grow lights will give off heat. Generally, the more powerful your lights, the more heat they give off. A small 100W LED might not make much heat, but a big 600W light can raise the temperature of a room by several degrees unless you [vent the heat out](https://www.growweedeasy.com/stealthy-exhaust-systems-for-indoor-plants).
If you want to install a lot of bright lights in a small space, you may need an air conditioner in addition to your exhaust system to make sure you keep your temps in the right range.
Some lights produce don’t produce much heat at all. If you’re growing just a few plants in a grow tent or box, you can use a fan to pull hot air away from the grow lights and into the room (or out a window) to keep plants cool enough.
I’ll help you find the right grow light for your space in [Step 2](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#Step-2-Choose-Your-Grow-Light).
_**Outdoor Growing**_
Growing outdoors costs far less money to get started since you don’t have to get grow lights or create an indoor grow area. Though you do have to worry about privacy/stealth, possible pollination, people stealing your plants, bugs, deer and other unexpected outdoor visitors.
Check out these outdoor [auto-flowering plants](https://www.growweedeasy.com/2024-guide-to-cannabis-auto-flowering-strains) by Raymond, one of our students from [Home Grow Masterclass](https://homegrowmasterclass.com/course).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/autos-Rainbow-Kush-on-either-end-and-two-Four-Assed-Monkeys-in-the-middle-cannabis-outdoor-plants-raymond-trophy-pic-home-grow-masterclass-sm.jpg)
However, if you pick the right strain and live in a good environment, it can be much cheaper to grow outdoors. That’s because outdoors, you don’t have to provide _everything_ for your plants. The sun will do a lot of the most cost-heavy work for you by providing a free grow light.
Of course, when you’re growing outside, it’s not always possible to control the environment perfectly. If it’s dry, you need to water your plants more. If it’s too rainy, you need to protect your plants from getting overwatered. Wind, bugs, and creatures can cause issues, and you have no control over the temperature.
When it comes to temperatures, a good rule of thumb about cannabis plants is if it’s too hot for you, it’s probably too hot for your plants. And just like humans, cannabis plants can die if exposed to freezing or too-hot temps.
So if you know it’s going to be extremely hot or cold where you live, you may need to take extra steps to protect your plants from the elements, like setting up a small greenhouse.
[Learn more about the difference between growing cannabis indoors vs outdoors](https://www.growweedeasy.com/indoors-vs-outdoors-growing-marijuana).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2012/11/outdoor-cannabs-silhouette.jpg)
## **Step 2: Choose Your Grow Light – What kind of light do you need to grow cannabis successfully?**
There are lots of different grow lights for cannabis, including:
**3 Best Grow Lights for Growing Cannabis**
  1. [**The Sun**](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#sun) – free, powerful light source for [outdoor growers](https://www.growweedeasy.com/beginner-guide-to-growing-cannabis-outdoors).
  2. **Big Bulb HID Grow Lights** – high-intensity but gets very hot, needs venting.
     * [Metal Halide (MH) & High-Pressure Sodium (HPS)](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#mh-hps)
     * [LEC (CMH) grow lights](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#lecs)
  3. [**LED Grow Lights**](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#leds) – **best option** for most indoor growers, energy-efficient and effective.

_Poor Cannabis Grow Lights (avoid)_
  * [Regular Household Light Bulbs](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#household-lighting)
  * [Fluorescent Lights (like a T5 grow light)](https://www.growweedeasy.com/10-step-cannabis-grow-guide#t5s)

**The Sun**
When you’re growing with the sun, you need to make sure that your plants are getting at least 8+ hours of direct sunlight each day for the best results.
It’s best that your plants get direct sunlight from at least 10am-4pm, and more light is better. Because of the high light needs of the cannabis plant (it needs more light than many other types of plants), it is not well suited to growing in a window. Though I’ve seen plenty of growers start their seeds in sunny windows before moving their plants to a more suitable final location.
[Learn how to grow cannabis outdoors](https://www.growweedeasy.com/beginner-guide-to-growing-cannabis-outdoors).
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/outdoor-cannabis-plant-looking-up-at-sun.jpg)
**Metal Halide (MH)& High-Pressure Sodium (HPS)**
Most often these are just called HPS grow lights. MH/HPS grow lights (like the light pictured below) are a type of “HID” light (“High Intensity Discharge” light). Whenever you see a cannabis grow light with one big light bulb, it’s likely an HID grow light. Besides LEDs, this is the most common type of light, especially for commercial growers that are growing cannabis indoors in a cooler climate. MH/HPS grow lights are surprisingly cheap to buy and set up, especially considering how incredibly powerful they are. For example, a [600W HPS grow light](https://www.amazon.com/iPower-Inch-Basic-Gull-Reflector/dp/B088LTMHG1?dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.rAbie4uYRGaX25JhaQwN9YJ2fRCHi8XUH09Wh1wvZ6Q1q2gkmhNhPzqJ9F_DYqoTDUFVR3uLbIUKAHY4qAjycrklBy867Hucv0gDMSjTWhDbTZZBa6RTSb2SmWRRvor9cNJyhppM2_FuPODQCAJJ_ThBRi-J4p6Hn9BSLOUuxc5Ct_Oy-p8PQfZZb2EHwp_ROZU2IFwwtS9mEswLRT9qfRD3ZJqNqmaxKI44E20YCvOepx1ZmTFK2AXiUevqM_yVktBCQ2IRUBwt7yHQnX-5Jc944fmYpO0xNQqCfXC5L21nG3ZrSXNjgaqxfpMX2_QExzz72qlp38qUQvBqmwOxYA-2hrJujZknPl1uRbIRpbUvCgWXjOItu0CdtS-sKPnpcAHYRwPuuXYwuWWDNRyZASTUvIN9CW9aI50EwKqwPMxYPwp4Hi88yTQKV4u_zSBV.dtdMl9S9pweidOIDo4rODovw2-C6EF0xIrz8ukF7mzc&dib_tag=se&keywords=600w%2Bhps%2Bgrow%2Blight&qid=1738970756&sr=8-6&ufe=app_do%3Aamzn1.fos.9fe8cbfa-bf43-43d1-a707-3f4e65a4b666&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=0105b8a7c6628b16a73faaebc106e6b6&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) costs less than $200 and can produce up to 2 lbs of weed. That’s cheaper than a comparable LED grow light to get started.
HID lights work very well for growing cannabis and produce consistently good yields and bud quality indoors. However, they run hotter than LEDs and use a lot of electricity, leaving a bigger mark on your electricity bill. The cost for electricity and cooling costs more over time compared to using LEDs. If you do go with MH/HPS grow lights, you definitely want to make sure you’re getting the exact right lights for your space so you don’t pay for more light than you really need. HID lighting (HPS in particular) has another problem in that it’s much less popular than a it used to be. While you used to be able to get them in a variety of sizes, typically now they only come in huge sizes (600W or 1000W) made for commercial growers.
HPS grow lights used to come in small sizes which didn’t get very hot.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/auto-flowering-plants-57-days-250w-hps-grow-light.jpg)
But now, HPS grow lights pretty much only comes in “extra large”, suitable for commercial growers.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/big-flowering-cannabis-plants-under-600w-hps-grow-light.jpg)
**LEC (CMH) grow lights**
LEC (Light Emitting Ceramic) is a brand name for a type of light (CMH – Ceramic Metal Halide). CMH grow lights are HIDs, which means they get **hot**. The technology has existed for quite a while (I remember first seeing CMH grow lights in the 2000s), but this type of grow light goes in and out of vogue every few years. Although they get very hot, they have some positive traits for growing cannabis. For one, LECs have a natural colored light that makes it easier to care for and diagnose plant problems. It’s overall better for security to have a light that doesn’t scream “WEED GROWING HERE!” like the unearthly yellow hue of an HPS or even the often-purple-tinted light of LEDs. One thing that’s unique is CMH produce significant levels of UV light, which results in significantly increased trichome production on buds. That makes them a good choice for growers looking to make extracts like hash, kief, rosin, etc. They don’t emit EMI (Electro-Magnetic Interference) as much as their noisy HPS cousins, which means you’re less likely to have a HAM radio enthusiast accidentally tracking down your grow. Plus, cannabis plants grow surprisingly fast under a 315 LEC, and produce impressive yields for the amount of electricity.
  * [Nebula grows 1+ pound of weed with a 315W LEC](https://www.growweedeasy.com/how-to-grow-cannabis-315-lec-grow-light)
  * [Are LEC Grow Lights Good for Growing Cannabis?](https://www.growweedeasy.com/lec-grow-lights-ceramic-metal-halide-cmh)

[](https://www.amazon.com/Sun-System-906298-Flower-Power/dp/B07GSB51L8?pd_rd_w=FLX8o&content-id=amzn1.sym.42aa4ac9-41cf-40f2-844a-256751b93dbf&pf_rd_p=42aa4ac9-41cf-40f2-844a-256751b93dbf&pf_rd_r=S7E5TCY9P9Q42T5JQZH9&pd_rd_wg=uymjk&pd_rd_r=78b37ba1-f41d-44b2-aaf8-b459a819e61f&pd_rd_i=B07GSB51L8&th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=65e6d96ff6c8a720633a52727bb5ff60&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl)
**LED grow lights – Best choice for most indoor growers!**
Modern LEDs are top-tier grow lights for growing cannabis indoors. LED grow lights are extremely energy-efficient and produce excellent cannabis yields, bud density, and overall potency. But it’s not just bud quality and size. LEDs use less electricity and give off less heat than other types of grow lights. They’re also easy to use, simplifying the grow experience for most indoor growers. Plus, in our [side-by-side tests](https://www.growweedeasy.com/side-by-side-cannabis-grow-journal-experiments-by-nebula-haze), LEDs often produce higher-THC buds than the same clones grown under [other grow lights like HPS](https://www.growweedeasy.com/rip-hps-grow-lights-leds-finally-won). So LEDs grow the most potent buds, too!
LEDs rock to grow weed! Check out our student Sandra from our [Home Grow Masterclass](https://homegrowmasterclass.com/course), who grew these incredible cannabis plants with a 300W [Mars Hydro TSL2000 LED](https://www.amazon.com/MARS-HYDRO-Spectrum-Hydroponic-Fixtures/dp/B07PLZLKVM?th=1&linkCode=ll1&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=6594ec5b1b64fee9604807bf83af540e&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) grow light.
[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/incredible-flowering-plants-using-led-grow-lights-by-sandra-sm.jpg)
LEDs are the only type of grow light that has seen major technological research and development in the past few decades of growing cannabis. And LED grow light technology is still improving every day for cannabis growers. As of 2025, LED manufacturers are still constantly fine-tuning their LEDs with extensive testing on live cannabis plants, resulting in faster growth, better bud quality, and bigger yields. Especially cannabis-focused companies like [HLG](https://www.amazon.com/stores/HorticultureLightingGroup/page/B13793D7-BD3F-4F4F-9FA7-0E91FD65F544?store_ref=bl_ast_dp_brandLogo_sto&linkCode=ll2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=af9f596098f116ce6bf6d82de19b9091&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl), [Mars Hydro](https://www.amazon.com/stores/page/AC8ADC82-7E1B-4465-8041-3D4EB4B70639?ingress=2&visitId=6fb541c3-75f8-41be-94d9-f0f42005d5f1&store_ref=bl_ast_dp_brandLogo_sto&linkCode=ll2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=535b70455e8184b12db55ada33f3ee2d&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl), and [Spider Farmer](https://www.amazon.com/stores/page/94D9EF15-2C5B-4EE5-B6BF-A867937FD72B?ingress=2&visitId=066b252c-6961-4462-a513-b2ae9843e9ad&store_ref=bl_ast_dp_brandLogo_sto&linkCode=ll2&tag=4200a7-20&linkId=b3d04b100eefeffcb0c38b27f01ad880&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl).
Each LED model is different and needs to be kept a different distance away from your plants. It can sometimes be hard to find any “standard” advice about growing with LEDs, yet these days many brands are well-tested and trusted by cannabis growers. These brands tend to have good support for cannabis-growing questions if you contact them directly. When in doubt, it’s always a good idea to just ask the manufacturer about how far away the lights should be kept, as that’s where new growers are most likely to mess up.
  * [Top 5 LED grow lights for growing weed](https://www.growweedeasy.com/5-best-led-grow-lights-2025-cannabis-yields-speed-bud-quality#hlg-blackbird-led-grow-light-for-cannabis)
  * [LED vs HPS Grow Lights](https://www.growweedeasy.com/rip-hps-grow-lights-leds-finally-won)

[](https://www.growweedeasy.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/01/grow-cannabis-home-led-grow-light-flowering-stage-big-buds.jpg)
[Get a detailed breakdown of all the different types of cannabis grow lights](https://www.growweedeasy.com/growing-marijuana-what-type-of-lights).
